medical_history.chief_complaint: rising LFTs while on treatment holiday
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: female with hx of orthotopic liver transplantation ___ years ago in ___ with humoral graft rejection, s/p treatment with plasmapheresis/IVIG x4 and rituxan x1 with improvement. Readmitted for worsening LFTs (bili from 4.5 (sun), 4.9 (mon) to 5.8(wed)) while on ___ holiday. Patient was recently admitted to ___ on ___ with abd pain, jaundice, and malaise, found to have humoral type liver rejection with Class II antibodies, not responsive to ATG and high dose steroids. Patient improved s/p plasmapheresis/IVIG x4 and rituxan x1 and was discharged home with close follow up. Patient also undergoing eval for re-transplantation, MELD 12. Patient currently feels well, no n/v/d/abd pain.
medical_history.past_medical_history: s/p liver transplant ___ ___ years ago, found to have acute humoral type rejection, anxiety, depression, h/o D&C for heavy menstrual periods, ? hidradenitis suppurativa
medical_history.family_history: cancer in her father, family history of anxiety and depression

physical_examination.general_examination: VS: 97.9 130/87 73 18 100%RA GENERAL - Alert, interactive, NAD HEENT - PERRL, EOMI, sclerae icteric, jaundice at bottom of tongue, MMM, OP clear NECK - Supple, no thyromegaly, no JVD HEART - PMI non-displaced, RRR, nl S1-S2, no MRG LUNGS - CTAB, no r/rh/wh, good air movement, resp unlabored, no accessory muscle use ABDOMEN - NABS, soft, diffusely mildly tender (worse at mid lower quadrant and RUQ) EXTREMITIES - WWP, no c/c/e, 2+ peripheral pulses
